passage: A university study examined the relationship between hours of weekly exercise and self-reported stress levels among college students. The researchers found that students who exercised more hours per week reported lower stress levels.
stem: Which choice most effectively uses data from the figure to support the researchers' finding?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Students exercising 7 or more hours per week reported an average stress level of 3.2 out of 10, while those exercising 3-6 hours reported 5.8 and those exercising 0-2 hours reported 7.4.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Students exercising 0-2 hours per week reported the highest stress level at 7.4 out of 10, which was above the overall average of 5.5.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Students exercising 3-6 hours per week reported an average stress level of 5.8, which was 1.6 points lower than the 0-2 hour group.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The difference in stress levels between the 3-6 hour group (5.8) and the 7+ hour group (3.2) was 2.6 points on a 10-point scale.

explanation: The claim is that more exercise correlates with lower stress. Choice A shows the full pattern across all three exercise levels (7+ hrs = 3.2, 3-6 hrs = 5.8, 0-2 hrs = 7.4), clearly demonstrating that stress decreases as exercise increases. Choice B mentions only the highest stress group. Choice C compares only two groups. Choice D compares only two groups and focuses on a point difference.

distractorRationale.A: Correct: shows all three exercise levels and their corresponding stress scores, demonstrating a clear inverse relationship.
distractorRationale.B: Wrong (incomplete evidence): cites only the least-active group and an overall average, failing to show the progression across all levels.
distractorRationale.C: Wrong (misread value): compares only two of three groups and focuses on the magnitude of difference rather than the overall trend.
distractorRationale.D: Wrong (incomplete evidence): compares only the middle and most-active groups without including the least-active group, which is needed to establish the full pattern.

imageDescription: Bar chart titled 'Average Self-Reported Stress Level by Weekly Exercise Hours.' X-axis: Weekly Exercise Hours (0-2 hours, 3-6 hours, 7+ hours). Y-axis: Average Stress Level (1-10 scale). Bars: 0-2 hours = 7.4, 3-6 hours = 5.8, 7+ hours = 3.2. Source: University Health Sciences Department, 2024.

chartData.title: Average Self-Reported Stress Level by Weekly Exercise Hours

passageMetadata.source: Adapted from University Health Sciences Department research study
